title: QUIC Interop Runner Multiconnect Sequential Dispatch Verification
verification_method: Local execution, artifact inspection, and report review.
preconditions: The local host can resolve and bind loopback addresses.
The test host can create temporary PEM files and qlog directories.
The managed QUIC runtime is available in the current .NET environment.
scope: Verify that the `multiconnect` testcase uses the real endpoint-host transfer path sequentially, one managed connection per URL, that malformed input fails before transport success is claimed, and that a stalled response fails the child process honestly instead of hanging behind the external runner timeout.
procedure: Run the new requirement-home test file or a narrow filter that selects `REQ_QUIC_INT_0015`.
Inspect the captured stdout, stderr, and qlog artifacts from the sequential multiconnect smoke run.
Confirm the output shows connection 1/2 and 2/2 markers instead of a generic unsupported exit.
Confirm malformed multiconnect input fails before transport success is claimed and does not produce qlog output.
Confirm the stalled-response negative coverage sends the HTTP/0.9 request line, then fails with a timeout-driven exit code `1` and no preserved partial download.

Run the local interop helper against `quic-go` with `-LocalRole client -PeerImplementationSlots quic-go -TestCases multiconnect`, then inspect the preserved run root when the helper reports advisory-only `FileNotFoundError` handling.

Confirm the live preserved `handshakeloss` output shows `completed managed multiconnect download` through the final `50/50` connection together with `client exited with code 0`, even if the upstream runner's own Markdown/JSON report still records a failed post-check result.
If the tree is temporarily mid-edit and a run fails for an unrelated build reason, wait briefly and rerun once to confirm the lane still behaves honestly after the transient issue clears.
expected_result: The local interop harness routes `multiconnect` through the real endpoint-host transfer path one URL at a time, the sequential smoke run completes with qlog artifacts, malformed input fails early without broadening the supported promise, a stalled response fails with exit code `1` before the outer runner timeout can mask the outcome, and the fresh client-role `quic-go` `handshakeloss` replay completes all managed downloads even if the upstream runner's post-check later throws an external `FileNotFoundError`.
status_summary: Passed; the requirement-home test lane confirmed sequential `multiconnect` dispatch, qlog capture, malformed-input rejection, and stalled-response honest failure on 2026-04-21, and the fresh client-role `quic-go` `handshakeloss` rerun on 2026-04-22 completed managed downloads through connection `50/50` with a clean local client exit at `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-100630446-client-chrome/`. The remaining upstream report failure is the external runner's post-check `FileNotFoundError`, not a managed multiconnect transport failure.
